assertions.rule: Use pgTable with correct column type imports from drizzle-orm/pg-core
assertions.with_skill_evidence: Imports pgTable, serial, text, integer, json, timestamp, index from 'drizzle-orm/pg-core' and uses pgTable()
assertions.without_skill_evidence: Imports pgTable, serial, text, integer, json from 'drizzle-orm/pg-core' and uses pgTable()

assertions.rule: Use $type<T>() for JSON columns instead of leaving as unknown/any
assertions.with_skill_evidence: Uses json('metadata').$type<{ color: string; weight: number }>()
assertions.without_skill_evidence: Uses json('metadata') without $type — left as unknown

assertions.rule: Use $inferSelect and $inferInsert for TypeScript type inference
assertions.with_skill_evidence: Uses typeof products.$inferSelect and typeof products.$inferInsert
assertions.without_skill_evidence: Uses InferSelectModel<typeof products> and InferInsertModel — the older API. Not $inferSelect/$inferInsert.

assertions.rule: Use camelCase for TS property names, snake_case for DB column names
assertions.with_skill_evidence: productId (camelCase) maps to 'product_id' (snake_case)
assertions.without_skill_evidence: Uses product_id as the TS property name (snake_case in TS), violating the naming convention

assertions.rule: Define indexes on foreign keys and frequently queried columns
assertions.with_skill_evidence: Defines index('reviews_product_id_idx').on(table.productId) on the FK column
assertions.without_skill_evidence: No indexes defined on any column

assertions.rule: Use relations() helper from drizzle-orm with one/many
assertions.with_skill_evidence: Imports relations from 'drizzle-orm' and defines usersRelations, postsRelations, tagsRelations, postsToTagsRelations
assertions.without_skill_evidence: Imports relations from 'drizzle-orm' and defines usersRelations, postsRelations, postTagsRelations

assertions.rule: Many-to-many requires explicit junction table with composite primaryKey
assertions.with_skill_evidence: Creates postsToTags table with primaryKey({ columns: [table.postId, table.tagId] })
assertions.without_skill_evidence: Creates postTags junction table but does NOT define a composite primaryKey — no third argument with primaryKey()

assertions.rule: Use db.query.X.findMany({ with: ... }) for relational queries
assertions.with_skill_evidence: Uses db.query.users.findMany({ with: { posts: { with: { tags: { with: { tag: true } } } } } })
assertions.without_skill_evidence: Uses manual leftJoin chains instead of db.query.users.findMany with 'with', requiring manual reshaping

assertions.rule: Import filter operators (eq, gt, like, and, inArray, etc.) from drizzle-orm
assertions.with_skill_evidence: Imports eq, gt, like, and, inArray, asc, sql from 'drizzle-orm'
assertions.without_skill_evidence: Imports eq, gt, like, and, inArray, desc, sql from 'drizzle-orm'

assertions.rule: Use sql template tag for raw SQL, never string concatenation
assertions.with_skill_evidence: Analytics query uses sql`...` tagged template with table references
assertions.without_skill_evidence: Uses sql`...` tagged template for analytics query

assertions.rule: Use cursor-based pagination instead of OFFSET for large datasets
assertions.with_skill_evidence: Uses gt(users.id, lastSeenId) with orderBy(asc(users.id)).limit(pageSize) — explicitly notes OFFSET scans skipped rows
assertions.without_skill_evidence: Uses .offset(page * pageSize) for the 10M row table

assertions.rule: Use db.transaction() for multi-step data modifications
assertions.with_skill_evidence: Both transferMoney and createUserWithProfile are wrapped in db.transaction(async (tx) => { ... })
assertions.without_skill_evidence: transferMoney uses db.transaction() but createUserWithProfile does NOT — runs two separate inserts outside a transaction

assertions.rule: Transaction auto-rolls back on error
assertions.with_skill_evidence: Explicitly explains: 'db.transaction() auto-rolls back on error. If any query inside the callback throws... the entire transaction is rolled back — no partial writes occur.'
assertions.without_skill_evidence: Does not explain auto-rollback. Says 'the transaction will handle it' vaguely, and for user+profile says 'you'd need to manually clean up' — showing lack of understanding

assertions.rule: Use drizzle-kit generate then drizzle-kit migrate for migration workflow
assertions.with_skill_evidence: Shows 'npx drizzle-kit generate' then 'npx drizzle-kit migrate' in sequence
assertions.without_skill_evidence: Shows 'npx drizzle-kit generate' and 'npx drizzle-kit migrate'

assertions.rule: Config object uses satisfies Config pattern
assertions.with_skill_evidence: Export ends with } satisfies Config;
assertions.without_skill_evidence: Uses const config: Config = { ... }; export default config; instead of satisfies Config

assertions.rule: Type JSON columns with $type<T>() - no any/unknown
assertions.with_skill_evidence: Uses json('metadata').$type<UserMetadata>() with a defined interface
assertions.without_skill_evidence: Uses json('metadata') without $type, then casts with 'as' at read time

assertions.rule: No raw SQL string building - always use sql template
assertions.with_skill_evidence: JSONB query uses sql`${users.metadata}->>'theme' = ${theme}`
assertions.without_skill_evidence: No raw SQL string concatenation present (no raw SQL used at all beyond eq)

assertions.rule: Select specific columns for large tables instead of select()
assertions.with_skill_evidence: Uses db.select({ id: users.id, email: users.email, name: users.name, createdAt: users.createdAt }) — explicitly excludes bio
assertions.without_skill_evidence: Uses db.select().from(users) — fetches all columns including the large bio for 500K rows

assertions.rule: Paginate production queries - never fetch all rows
assertions.with_skill_evidence: Uses cursor pagination with gt(users.id, lastSeenId) and .limit(pageSize)
assertions.without_skill_evidence: Uses db.select().from(users) with no limit or pagination — fetches all 500K rows

assertions.rule: Reuse connection in serverless - cache db instance outside handler
assertions.with_skill_evidence: cachedDb declared outside handler, getDb() returns cached instance, pool max: 1
assertions.without_skill_evidence: Creates new Pool inside every handler invocation, then calls pool.end() — no connection reuse
